::: {.page-container}

::: {.content-page}

# CV

::: {.cv-hero}

::: {.cv-hero-text}

I am a postdoctoral researcher in social epidemiology, with a focus on social inequalities in health, life course epidemiology, preventive healthcare, and complexity-informed approaches to health systems.

:::

::: {.cv-download-box}

[Download full CV](assets/cv/Cornelia_Wagner_CV.pdf){.button-primary}

::: {.cv-note}
PDF · Updated periodically
:::

:::

:::

## Current position

::: {.cv-item}

### Postdoctoral researcher

Population Health Laboratory, University of Fribourg, Switzerland

:::

## Research interests

- Social inequalities in health
- Life course epidemiology
- Preventive healthcare
- Healthcare services research
- Complexity science
- Longitudinal methods and sequence analysis

## Selected academic activities

::: {.cv-list}

::: {.cv-item}

### Publications

My work has been published in journals including *The Lancet Public Health*, *European Journal of Public Health*, and *SSM - Population Health*.

:::

::: {.cv-item}

### Teaching and supervision

I contribute to teaching in epidemiology, public health, and community health.

:::

::: {.cv-item}

### Research agenda

I am developing an independent research programme on unequal preventive healthcare trajectories, connecting life course epidemiology, healthcare services research, and complexity science.

:::

:::

:::

:::
